bioHotLightweight Curation — AI selection & significance rating
← bioHot

Metagenomics & Microbiome Informatics

Computational methods for metagenomic assembly, binning, taxonomic profiling, and functional annotation of microbial communities.

20 papers curated · High: 4 · Medium: 16 · Low: 0 · Updated: 2026-07-10

bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

A deep-learning-informed prior and Bayesian model for differential AP-MS interactome analysis

This paper presents BayesInteractomics, a new computational framework that combines deep learning with Bayesian statistics to improve the analysis of affinity-purification mass spectrometry (AP-MS) data. By using a neural network trained on protein structures to predict direct binding and incorporating this as prior knowledge, the method better distinguishes genuine protein interactions from background noise compared to existing tools like SAINTexpress and CompPASS.

proteomicsmachine-learningtool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

scJET: Full-gene Space Single-cell Expression Generation with Patch-based Transformer Modeling

scJET is a new generative model for single-cell RNA-seq data that works with all genes, not just a subset of highly variable ones. It uses a patch-based Transformer to denoise and generate full transcriptome data while preserving both global and local biological structure. This approach offers a more complete and efficient way to model single-cell expression at the whole-genome scale.

single-celltranscriptomicsdeep-learning
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

SPECTER-Based Semantic Triage of Biomedical Literature for Systematic Reviews in Mutational Signature Analysis

This paper presents a reproducible pipeline that uses SPECTER scientific-document embeddings and similarity metrics to rank biomedical literature for systematic reviews, specifically in mutational signature analysis. The approach outperforms keyword-only and TF-IDF methods in screening efficiency, reducing the manual review burden.

machine-learningtoolmethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

BBBP_Atlas: Unified Interpretable Modeling of Blood Brain Barrier Permeability across Small Molecules and Peptides

This paper introduces BBBP_Atlas, a unified framework for predicting blood-brain barrier permeability across both small molecules and peptides, addressing limitations of existing models that are often restricted to small molecules or require predefined descriptors. The authors compile and standardize fragmented public datasets to reduce data leakage risk, and develop an interpretable model that improves transferability across chemically diverse modalities.

drug-discoverymachine-learningdatabase
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Mutational bias shapes protein evolution across RNA viruses

This study shows that mutational biases, not just natural selection, play a major role in shaping protein evolution in RNA viruses. Using SARS-CoV-2, the authors demonstrate that the virus's mutation spectrum explains over half of the observed amino acid changes and predicts long-term shifts in protein composition during the pandemic.

evolutiongenomicsmethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Metabolic Rewiring in Triple-Negative Breast Cancer: Systems Analysis of TCGA-BRCA Transcriptome Reveals Prognostic Hub Genes

This study uses a systems biology approach to analyze metabolic rewiring in triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC), identifying key hub genes and pathways from TCGA-BRCA transcriptome data. The authors find ATP1A2 as a significant prognostic marker, highlighting its potential role in TNBC progression and patient stratification.

systems-biologytranscriptomicscancer
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

Learning the spatial cell-cell communication network to decode multi-channel signaling and predict network-hub vulnerabilities with MOSANIC

MOSANIC is a new computational method that learns spatial cell-cell communication networks from multi-channel imaging data, enabling the identification of signaling hubs, relays, and network vulnerabilities. By modeling the entire signaling network rather than individual interactions, it can predict how tissue-wide communication responds to perturbations such as the loss of a single cell type. This approach provides a systems-level understanding of intercellular signaling in tissues and identifies rational intervention points for disease.

spatial-omicsnetwork-analysismethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Assessing tensor decomposition quality of immune profiling data from a dictionary learning perspective

This paper presents new metrics for assessing the quality of non-negative tensor decompositions applied to immune profiling data, helping researchers choose the optimal rank and trial. The authors show that considering the underlying experimental data model can guide the development of these metrics, and they reframe the results through a dictionary learning perspective.

methodologymachine-learningimmunology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Generative AI Models Reveal Dynamic Views of Aging (DyViA) Phenotypes in Healthy Individuals

This paper introduces DyViA, a generative AI platform that uses diffusion and GAN models to predict personalized aging trajectories for healthy individuals. The platform can forecast how specific phenotypes (e.g., biomarkers) may change over time, offering a new tool for studying healthy aging.

machine-learningdeep-learningaging
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

A five-dimensional functional state space for fingerprinting disease transcriptomes

This paper introduces a five-dimensional functional state space framework that maps disease transcriptomes onto coordinated activity patterns across major biological systems, enabling easier comparison across conditions and human-AI interpretation. The framework organizes transcriptomic signals into five functional systems, 14 subcategories, and an infrastructure layer, implemented as a reproducible pipeline for functional scoring.

transcriptomicssystems-biologymethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Gene-specific exponent-corrected normalization for library size in bulk RNA-seq

This paper shows that standard library size normalization methods for bulk RNA-seq often fail to fully remove correlation between gene expression and sequencing depth in large datasets. The authors propose a gene-specific exponent-corrected normalization approach that more effectively eliminates this technical artifact. This could improve the reliability of differential expression analyses across large-scale studies.

transcriptomicsmethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

IgGM2: An All-Atom Foundation Model for Adaptive Immune Receptor Design

IgGM2 is a new AI model that can simultaneously design the sequence and 3D structure of immune receptors like antibodies and T-cell receptors, which is more accurate than previous methods that handled these tasks separately. It uses a 'structure-to-design' approach to model how amino acid sequence, atomic conformation, and target binding fit together across different receptor types.

deep-learningprotein-structureimmunology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

Gene Program Negotiation Defines Cellular Identity in Single-Cell Transcriptomes

This paper introduces Gene Program Negotiation (GPN), a graph-based computational framework that models how multiple simultaneously active gene programs collectively determine cellular identity in single-cell transcriptomes, overcoming the limitations of existing methods that analyze programs independently. By reconstructing cell-specific program interactions, GPN provides deeper insight into regulatory decision-making and cellular heterogeneity.

single-celltranscriptomicsmethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Rectangle: robust and scalable multiscale deconvolution informed by single-cell RNA sequencing data

Rectangle is a new Python framework for deconvolving bulk RNA-seq data using single-cell references. It improves resolution of closely related cell types, scales efficiently to large datasets, and accounts for cell types not present in the reference.

transcriptomicssingle-celltool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Mind the Alignment Gap: A Spatial Transcriptomics Benchmark for Scientific Coding Agents

This paper presents a new framework for automatically building benchmarks to evaluate scientific coding agents, using spatial transcriptomics alignment as a case study. The authors created 40 tasks from peer-reviewed papers where agents must produce coordinate tables, addressing the challenge of verifying outputs in scientific research tasks. This work offers a path to faster and more systematic evaluation of AI agents in computational biology.

spatial-omicstoolmachine-learning
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Coding agents author interpretable single-cell embedding models from the literature

This paper introduces a method where coding agents automatically generate interpretable single-cell embedding models by extracting marker-gene programs from the literature, bypassing the need for post-hoc interpretation and batch correction. The approach uses a structured Python template that the agent edits to curate named, literature-cited gene programs for a chosen biological subdomain. This provides a novel way to leverage prior knowledge from the literature to create more interpretable and biologically meaningful embeddings.

single-cellmachine-learningtool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

LeafRank: A phylodynamic framework for inferring relative fitness from single-cell phylogenies in chromosomally unstable tumors

LeafRank is a new computational method that uses single-cell DNA sequencing data to infer the relative fitness of individual cancer cells in tumors with chromosomal instability. By modeling tumor evolution as a branching process, it can quantify which cells are more likely to drive tumor growth, even in complex scenarios like whole-genome duplication.

cancersingle-celltool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

EZSolver: Template-free prediction of polar enzymatic mechanisms via bidirectional flow matching and search

EZSolver is a new computational tool that predicts how enzymes catalyze reactions without relying on pre-defined reaction templates, using a generative AI approach called flow matching. It can generalize to novel chemical reactions that existing rule-based methods fail to handle, which could accelerate enzyme design and discovery.

machine-learningmethodologystructural-biology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

BertST: BERT-based Spatial Domain Identification in Patient Data

This paper introduces BertST, a BERT-based model for identifying spatial domains in spatial transcriptomics data. Unlike existing methods that rely on Graph Neural Networks, BertST uses a Transformer architecture to jointly model local and long-range spatial dependencies, improving the accuracy of tissue region partitioning.

spatial-omicstranscriptomicsdeep-learning
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Thematic Shifts in Early-High-Impact Cancer Genomics and Diagnostics Research: A Bibliometric and Semantic Analysis

This study uses a bibliometric and semantic analysis to identify the most influential recent publications in cancer genomics and diagnostics, revealing thematic shifts in topics that attract early citation prominence. The findings can inform research strategy and clinical translation by highlighting emerging high-impact areas.

cancergenomicsmachine-learning
